faq.question: What is the golden ratio?
faq.answer: The golden ratio (φ ≈ 1.618) is a mathematical proportion found in nature and art. Two quantities are in the golden ratio when their ratio equals the ratio of their sum to the larger quantity.

faq.question: How do I use the golden ratio in photography?
faq.answer: Place key subjects at golden ratio points (about 38.2% and 61.8% of the frame). This creates more dynamic compositions than centering, similar to but more refined than the rule of thirds.

faq.question: Is the golden ratio better than the rule of thirds?
faq.answer: They're similar — the rule of thirds divides at 33%/67%, while the golden ratio divides at 38.2%/61.8%. The golden ratio places subjects slightly closer to center, which many find more pleasing.
